pregunta: As airflow moves towards trailing edge, boundary layer becomes
opciones: remains constant in thickness and flow type
explicacion: As air flows over a surface, the boundary layer grows in thickness due to friction, and laminar flow transitions into turbulent flow toward the trailing edge.

pregunta: What is aileron differential?
opciones: Ailerons move equally up and down
Ailerons move up more than down
Ailerons move only at high speed
explicacion: Aileron differential reduces adverse yaw by having the up-going aileron deflect more than the down-going aileron.

pregunta: What keeps the nose wheel from getting cocked before entering the wheel well?
explicacion: A centering cam aligns the nose wheel straight before retraction into the wheel well.

pregunta: What is the angle of the wing to the horizontal plane?
explicacion: The dihedral angle is the upward angle of the wings relative to the horizontal plane.

pregunta: Why do we have to ground electrical equipment?
explicacion: Electrical equipment is grounded to reduce radio frequency interference and provide a safe path for unwanted electrical currents.

pregunta: What is the maximum size dope patch allowed?
explicacion: The maximum allowable size for a dope patch is typically 16 inches in any direction.

pregunta: What is the purpose of the flapper valve in an integral fuel tank?
opciones: To prevent the fuel from flowing away from the boost pumps
To cool the fuel before entering the engine
explicacion: Flapper valves help keep fuel around the boost pump area and prevent fuel from flowing away during aircraft maneuvers.

pregunta: Where is the cooling air taken from for a Freon system (vapor cycle air conditioning system) on the ground?
explicacion: On the ground, a vapor cycle (Freon) cooling system uses ambient air supplied by a cooling fan.

pregunta: What absorbs the first shock in an oleo during landing?
opciones: Oil transfers from the oil chamber into the air chamber around the tapered metering pin
explicacion: The initial landing shock is absorbed as hydraulic oil flows through the metering orifice around the tapered metering pin into the air chamber.

pregunta: The difference in wash-in and wash-out of an aircraft compensates for:
explicacion: Wash-in and wash-out differences are used to help compensate for propeller torque effects and improve stall characteristics.

pregunta: Where is the buttock line or buttline of an aircraft?
opciones: A height measurement above or below the horizontal reference line
A length measurement forward or aft of the datum
A width measurement left or right of, and parallel to the vertical centerline
A measurement along the wing chord from leading edge to trailing edge
explicacion: A buttock line is a width reference line measured left or right of the aircraft vertical centerline.

pregunta: Where does the last stage of cooling in an air cycle air conditioning system occur?
explicacion: In an air cycle system, the final cooling occurs as air expands through the expansion turbine.

pregunta: Grounding is electrically connecting a conductive object to the primary structure in order to prevent:
opciones: Development of radio frequency and static buildup
explicacion: Grounding helps prevent radio frequency interference and static electricity buildup by providing an electrical path to the primary structure.

pregunta: What is the purpose of the compensating port in a brake master cylinder?
opciones: To increase brake pressure during landing
To relieve excess pressure or fluid volume back to the reservoir when the brake is released
To prevent air from entering the brake line during bleeding
To lock the brake piston in the applied position
explicacion: The compensating port allows excess pressure or fluid volume to return to the reservoir when the brake is released.

pregunta: If an aircraft is at 8000 feet and the cabin pressurization is set on manual at 3500 feet, and the aircraft climbs 500 feet, what cabin pressure altitude will remain indicated?
explicacion: If the cabin pressurization is manually set to maintain 3500 feet, the cabin pressure altitude remains at 3500 feet while the aircraft climbs, assuming the system can maintain it.

pregunta: A fully integrated autopilot controls the aircraft around how many axes?
explicacion: A fully integrated autopilot controls the aircraft around three axes: pitch, roll, and yaw.

pregunta: Micron-type filters in a hydraulic system are designed to prevent the passage of solid particles greater than:
explicacion: Micron-type hydraulic filters are commonly used to prevent passage of solid particles greater than 10 microns.

pregunta: For most airfoil sections, if the chord of an aircraft wing is 100 inches, the most forward center of pressure will be located approximately:
explicacion: For many airfoils, the most forward center of pressure is approximately 30% of the chord. For a 100-inch chord, that is about 30 inches from the leading edge.

pregunta: What is the aspect ratio of a wing with a span of 24 feet and an area of 144 square feet?
explicacion: Aspect ratio = span² ÷ wing area = 24² ÷ 144 = 576 ÷ 144 = 4.

pregunta: What type of load occurs when forward pressure is applied to the elevator control and centrifugal force acts opposite to the force of weight?
explicacion: A negative load occurs when the aircraft is subjected to acceleration opposite to the normal force of weight, such as during forward elevator pressure.

pregunta: Smooth and easy response of an airplane to its controls is called:
explicacion: Controllability is the quality that allows an aircraft to respond smoothly and easily to control inputs.

pregunta: What two components make up a shimmy damper?
explicacion: A shimmy damper system commonly consists of a cam mechanism and a damping assembly to reduce nose wheel oscillations.

pregunta: What is the function of the compensator port in a brake master cylinder?
opciones: To trap hydraulic pressure in the brake line
To permit reservoir fluid to enter the master cylinder
To cool the brake fluid during operation
explicacion: The compensator port allows hydraulic fluid from the reservoir to enter the master cylinder and also relieves excess pressure when brakes are released.

pregunta: Which valve installed in a hydraulic system normally has the highest pressure setting?
explicacion: Thermal relief valves are set at very high pressures to protect the system from pressure increases caused by thermal expansion of trapped fluid.

pregunta: A multi-disk brake system is sluggish and jerky during operation. What corrective action should be taken?
explicacion: Dirty or sticking automatic adjusters can cause sluggish and jerky brake operation in multi-disk brake systems.

pregunta: Proximity sensors are inductive coils that operate in conjunction with:
explicacion: Inductive proximity sensors detect the presence of metal objects such as steel target plates.

pregunta: What component reduces oxygen pressure from the storage bottle to the pilot supply system?
explicacion: A pressure reducing valve lowers the high bottle pressure to a safe and usable pressure for the oxygen system.

pregunta: In monocoque construction, primary bending loads are carried by the:
explicacion: In monocoque construction, the skin carries most of the structural loads, including bending stresses.

pregunta: Form drag is classified as:
explicacion: Form drag is a type of parasite drag caused by the shape and airflow separation around an object.

pregunta: Where does a stall normally occur first on a wing?
explicacion: Aircraft wings are designed so the wing root stalls first, maintaining aileron effectiveness near the tips.

pregunta: The landing gear and their doors are controlled by a:
explicacion: Sequence valves control the order of operation of landing gear and gear doors during extension and retraction.

pregunta: When checking cable tension with a tensiometer, the correct _____ must be used for the specified cable size.
explicacion: The correct riser must be installed on a tensiometer to accurately measure the tension of a specific cable size.

pregunta: Aircraft components are bonded primarily to:
explicacion: Bonding prevents the buildup of static electricity by electrically connecting aircraft components together.

pregunta: What type of valve automatically directs fluid from a normal or emergency source to an actuating cylinder?
explicacion: A shuttle valve automatically selects fluid from either the normal or emergency hydraulic source.

pregunta: Why are tubes in deicer boots inflated alternately?
opciones: To keep disturbance of airflow to a minimum
To prevent ice from melting too quickly
explicacion: Alternating inflation of deicer boot tubes minimizes airflow disturbance while breaking ice accumulation.

pregunta: An airplane with good longitudinal stability should have a minimum tendency:
explicacion: Longitudinal stability refers to stability about the lateral axis and resistance to pitching motions.

pregunta: Which two functions are commonly used to control altitude in an autopilot system?
explicacion: Altitude hold maintains a selected altitude, while altitude select captures and levels the aircraft at a preselected altitude.

pregunta: What is the formula for wing loading?
opciones: Aircraft maximum gross weight ÷ total wing area
explicacion: Wing loading is calculated by dividing the aircraft maximum gross weight by the total wing area.

pregunta: A _____ reduces the crossflow on a wing, thereby reducing the strength of the trailing vortex.
explicacion: A wing fence reduces spanwise airflow (crossflow) on the wing, helping control airflow and reducing vortex strength.

pregunta: What are the two common types of AC motors used in aircraft?
explicacion: Induction and synchronous motors are the two most common types of AC motors used in aircraft systems.

pregunta: What are the cabin pressurization modes of operation?
explicacion: Typical cabin pressurization modes include constant differential, unpressurized, and isobaric operation.

pregunta: Which cabin pressurization mode maintains a constant cabin altitude regardless of aircraft altitude?
explicacion: In isobaric mode, the cabin pressure altitude is maintained constant even as aircraft altitude changes.

pregunta: The pressure required to service aircraft wheels and tires is specified in the:
explicacion: Aircraft wheel and tire servicing pressures are specified in the Aircraft Maintenance Manual (AMM).

pregunta: Prior to installing a tire, the bead seat of the wheel should be cleaned with:
explicacion: The bead seat area should be cleaned with isopropyl alcohol to remove contaminants before tire installation.

pregunta: Phosphate ester-based hydraulic fluid spilled on aircraft tires should be washed off with:
explicacion: Soap and water are used to remove phosphate ester hydraulic fluids such as Skydrol from tires and painted surfaces.

pregunta: What is the best safeguard against heat buildup in aircraft tires?
opciones: Short ground rolls, slow taxi speeds, minimum braking, and proper tire inflation
explicacion: Minimizing braking and taxi heat while maintaining proper tire inflation helps prevent excessive tire heat buildup.

pregunta: What is Station 0 on an aircraft?
explicacion: Station 0 is the aircraft datum reference line from which all horizontal measurements are taken.

pregunta: Communication between an aircraft and a ground station controller over long distances is commonly accomplished using:
explicacion: HF communication systems are commonly used for long-range aircraft-to-ground communication.

pregunta: In an AC generator system, aircraft batteries are charged by:
explicacion: Rectifiers convert AC power into DC power for charging aircraft batteries.

pregunta: What two modes are important in aircraft electrical load analysis?
explicacion: Electrical load analysis considers both continuous loads and intermittent loads to properly size the aircraft electrical system.

pregunta: During inspection of terminal strips in an aircraft electrical system, it should be determined that:
opciones: Terminal studs are free to rotate
Terminal studs are anchored against rotation
Terminal studs are lubricated with grease
Terminal studs are painted for identification
explicacion: Terminal studs must be anchored against rotation to prevent loosening and damage to electrical connections.

pregunta: The removal of air from an aircraft hydraulic system is generally accomplished:
opciones: By operating the various hydraulic components through several cycles
By increasing system pressure to maximum
explicacion: Cycling hydraulic components helps purge trapped air from the system.

pregunta: Two actuating cylinders of different diameters but the same stroke are connected to the same hydraulic pressure source. They will exert:
opciones: Different internal pressure but the same force
The same internal pressure but different amounts of force
The same force and same fluid volume
Different pressure and different stroke length
explicacion: Hydraulic pressure is the same throughout the system, but force depends on piston area (F = P × A).

pregunta: When one wheel locks up during deceleration, the antiskid system:
opciones: Applies maximum hydraulic pressure to the wheel
Dumps all hydraulic pressure on the locked wheel until it spins again
Locks the opposite wheel for balance
explicacion: An antiskid system reduces or dumps brake pressure on a locked wheel until rotation resumes.

pregunta: Which instrument indicates that an aircraft has properly coordinated rudder and aileron control?
opciones: Ball inclinometer in the turn-and-bank indicator
explicacion: The inclinometer ball indicates whether a turn is coordinated by showing slip or skid conditions.

pregunta: Which instruments are connected to an aircraft pitot-static system?
opciones: Turn coordinator, heading indicator, and VSI
Vertical speed indicator, altimeter, and airspeed indicator
Attitude indicator, VSI, and magnetic compass
explicacion: The pitot-static system supplies pressure information to the VSI, altimeter, and airspeed indicator.

pregunta: In a pitot-static system, the maximum altitude loss permitted during an aircraft instrument static pressure test is:
explicacion: The allowable leakage rate during a static system test is commonly limited to 100 feet per minute.

pregunta: Which component provides manual maneuverability of the aircraft while the autopilot is engaged?
explicacion: The flight controller allows the pilot to manually maneuver the aircraft while the autopilot remains engaged.

pregunta: Which tab moves in the same direction as the flight control surface?
explicacion: A servo tab moves in the same direction as the primary flight control surface to assist pilot movement.

pregunta: A conical elastomeric bearing is designed to carry:
explicacion: Conical elastomeric bearings are capable of supporting both high axial and radial loads while also damping vibration.

pregunta: During autorotation, seizing of the freewheeling unit would:
opciones: Allow the engine to drive the tail rotor only
Allow the main rotor to drive the engine
explicacion: If the freewheeling unit seizes during autorotation, the main rotor can drive the engine, defeating the purpose of autorotation.

pregunta: If the amplitude of vibration remains constant throughout the RPM range, the rotor condition is likely:
explicacion: Chordwise imbalance typically produces a vibration with constant amplitude across RPM ranges.

pregunta: Translational lift in a helicopter becomes noticeable at approximately:
explicacion: Effective translational lift is generally noticeable around 15 to 20 mph.

pregunta: To counteract main rotor torque, most European helicopters require:
explicacion: Most European helicopters rotate opposite to American helicopters and therefore require right pedal to counter torque.

pregunta: What type of pump is commonly used in a high-pressure hydraulic system?
explicacion: Piston pumps are commonly used in high-pressure hydraulic systems because of their efficiency and pressure capability.

pregunta: A dual tachometer on a single-engine helicopter indicates:
opciones: Main rotor RPM and tail rotor RPM
Engine RPM and main rotor RPM
Tail rotor RPM and turbine outlet temperature
explicacion: A dual tachometer displays both engine RPM and main rotor RPM.

pregunta: During autorotation, the airflow through the rotor system is:
explicacion: In autorotation, air flows upward through the rotor system, keeping the rotor turning.

pregunta: A semirigid rotor system is underslung primarily:
explicacion: Semirigid rotor systems are underslung to reduce stress and improve stability during flapping motion.

pregunta: An overstable helicopter with delayed control response may result from:
opciones: Soft stabilizer bar or soft dampers
explicacion: Soft dampers or a soft stabilizer bar can cause sluggish control response and overstable characteristics.

pregunta: A rotor system with hinges at the root of each blade permitting vertical movement is called:
explicacion: A fully articulated rotor system uses hinges that allow blades to flap independently up and down.

pregunta: A vibration related to rotor blade track is generally:
explicacion: Blade tracking problems commonly produce a once-per-revolution (1:1) vertical vibration.

pregunta: Dynamic balancing of a helicopter tail rotor is commonly accomplished using:
explicacion: Chadwick-Helmuth systems such as Strobex and Vibrex are used for rotor dynamic balancing.

pregunta: In a hydraulic boosted flight control system, artificial feel is commonly provided by:
explicacion: A gradient unit provides artificial feel in boosted flight control systems.

pregunta: The Coriolis effect in helicopters is associated with:
explicacion: The Coriolis effect causes blades to speed up or slow down in their lead-lag motion as blade mass moves closer or farther from the axis of rotation.

pregunta: A lateral vibration in a helicopter is commonly caused by:
explicacion: Chordwise imbalance can create lateral vibration in helicopter rotor systems.

pregunta: Moveable horizontal stabilizers on helicopters are primarily used:
opciones: To improve controllability and extend the center of gravity range
explicacion: Moveable horizontal stabilizers improve controllability and allow a wider CG operating range.

pregunta: A helicopter horizontal stabilizer is commonly shaped as an:
explicacion: Helicopter horizontal stabilizers are often designed as inverted asymmetrical airfoils.

pregunta: After alignment of a tail rotor driveshaft, runout is normally checked using a:
explicacion: A dial indicator is commonly used to check driveshaft runout.

pregunta: Ground resonance is primarily associated with:
explicacion: Ground resonance is a dangerous vibration condition associated mainly with fully articulated rotor systems.

pregunta: Tracking helicopter rotor blades is normally accomplished using:
explicacion: Rotor blade tracking is generally performed at constant rotor RPM and collective setting.

pregunta: Vertical flight of a helicopter is controlled primarily by:
explicacion: Collective pitch changes blade angle equally and controls vertical lift.

pregunta: A tail rotor may fail to compensate for main rotor torque if:
opciones: The tail rotor is incorrectly rigged
explicacion: Incorrect tail rotor rigging can prevent adequate anti-torque thrust from being produced.

pregunta: Pressure required to operate a helicopter rotor brake is usually supplied by:
opciones: The main hydraulic flight control system
explicacion: Rotor brakes commonly use an independent hydraulic source.

pregunta: The condition caused by advancing and retreating rotor blades is called:
explicacion: Dissymmetry of lift results from unequal lift between advancing and retreating rotor blades.

pregunta: Tail rotor vibrations are usually characterized as:
opciones: High-frequency vibrations felt in the pedals
explicacion: Tail rotor vibrations are typically high-frequency vibrations transmitted through the pedals.

pregunta: Rotor blades are permitted to flap primarily to compensate for:
explicacion: Blade flapping compensates for dissymmetry of lift between advancing and retreating blades.

pregunta: Damage due to mast bumping occurs primarily in:
explicacion: Mast bumping is primarily associated with semirigid rotor systems.

pregunta: A helicopter changes course in forward flight primarily by:
explicacion: Directional changes are made by tilting the main rotor disc using cyclic control.

pregunta: Cyclic control inputs are applied ahead of the desired rotor response because of:
explicacion: Due to gyroscopic precession, rotor response occurs approximately 90 degrees later in the direction of rotation.

pregunta: Variation of rotor blade pitch equally on all blades is controlled by:
explicacion: Collective pitch control changes the pitch angle equally on all rotor blades.

pregunta: For a helicopter in vertical flight at a constant rate, the relationship between forces is:
opciones: Thrust and lift greater than weight and drag
Lift equal to weight and drag balanced
explicacion: In steady vertical flight at a constant rate, lift equals weight and forces remain balanced.

pregunta: What kind of tab follows the movement of the control surface but in the opposite direction?
explicacion: An anti-servo (or balance) tab moves in the opposite direction of the control surface to increase control forces and improve stability.

pregunta: What is aileron differential?
opciones: Ailerons move equally up and down
Ailerons move up more than down
Ailerons move down more than up
explicacion: Aileron differential reduces adverse yaw by allowing the up-going aileron to deflect more than the down-going aileron.

pregunta: What keeps the nose wheel centered before entering the wheel well?
explicacion: A centering cam aligns the nose wheel straight before it retracts into the wheel well.

pregunta: What is the angle of the wing to the horizontal plane?
explicacion: The dihedral angle is the upward angle of the wings relative to the aircraft's horizontal plane.

pregunta: Why do we have to ground electrical equipment?
explicacion: Grounding provides a safe electrical path and minimizes static electricity and radio frequency interference.

pregunta: What is the maximum allowable size of a dope patch?
explicacion: The maximum allowable size of a dope patch is 16 inches in any direction.

pregunta: What is the purpose of the flapper valve in an integral fuel tank?
opciones: To prevent the fuel from flowing away from the boost pumps
explicacion: Flapper valves help retain fuel around the boost pumps during aircraft maneuvers.

pregunta: Where is the cooling air taken from for a Freon (vapor-cycle) air conditioning system while the aircraft is on the ground?
explicacion: On the ground, the condenser cooling fan draws ambient air through the vapor-cycle air conditioning system.

pregunta: What absorbs the first shock in an oleo strut during landing?
opciones: Oil transfers from the oil chamber into the air chamber around the tapered metering pin
explicacion: The initial impact is absorbed as hydraulic oil flows through the metering orifice around the tapered metering pin into the air chamber.

pregunta: If a propeller is turning clockwise (viewed from the cockpit), how is the torque created by rotation of the propeller counteracted?
explicacion: Wash-in of the left wing helps compensate for the rolling tendency produced by clockwise propeller torque.

pregunta: Where is the buttock line or buttline of an aircraft?
opciones: A height measurement above or below the horizontal reference line
A length measurement forward or aft of the datum
A width measurement left or right of, and parallel to the vertical centerline
A measurement along the wing chord
explicacion: A buttock line is a width reference measured left or right of, and parallel to, the aircraft vertical centerline.

pregunta: Where does the last stage of cooling in an air cycle air conditioning system occur?
explicacion: The final cooling in an air cycle system occurs as the air expands through the expansion turbine.

pregunta: Grounding electrically connects a conductive object to the primary structure in order to prevent:
opciones: Development of radio frequency interference and static buildup
explicacion: Grounding prevents the accumulation of static electricity and minimizes radio frequency interference.

pregunta: What is the purpose of the compensating port in a brake master cylinder?
opciones: To allow air to escape during bleeding
To relieve excess pressure or fluid back to the reservoir when the brake is released
explicacion: The compensating port relieves excess pressure and returns excess fluid to the reservoir when the brakes are released.

pregunta: If an aircraft is at 8,000 feet and the cabin pressurization is manually set to 3,500 feet, then the aircraft climbs another 500 feet, what cabin pressure altitude will remain indicated?
explicacion: In manual mode, if the system is set to maintain a cabin altitude of 3,500 feet, the cabin remains at that pressure altitude while the aircraft climbs.

pregunta: A fully integrated autopilot controls the aircraft around how many axes?
explicacion: A fully integrated autopilot controls the aircraft in pitch, roll, and yaw (three axes).

pregunta: Micron-type hydraulic filters are designed to prevent the passage of solid particles larger than:
explicacion: Many aircraft hydraulic systems use 10-micron filters to prevent damaging contaminants from circulating.

pregunta: For most airfoil sections, if the wing chord is 100 inches, the most forward center of pressure is located approximately:
explicacion: For many conventional airfoils, the most forward center of pressure is approximately 30% of the chord.

pregunta: What is the formula for aspect ratio?
explicacion: Aspect ratio is calculated as the square of the wing span divided by the wing area (AR = b²/S).

pregunta: What type of load occurs when forward elevator control input causes centrifugal force to act opposite to the force of weight?
explicacion: A negative load occurs when the aircraft experiences acceleration opposite to the normal direction of weight, such as during negative-G maneuvers.

pregunta: Smooth and easy response of an airplane to its controls is called:
explicacion: Controllability is the ability of an aircraft to respond smoothly and easily to pilot control inputs.

pregunta: What two components make up a hydraulic shimmy damper?
explicacion: The most common hydraulic shimmy damper consists of a piston operating inside a cylinder. (Some designs use vane-type dampers, but piston-and-cylinder is the standard answer.)

pregunta: What is the function of the compensator port in a brake master cylinder?
opciones: To permit reservoir fluid to enter the master cylinder
To trap pressure in the brake lines
explicacion: The compensating port allows fluid to enter the master cylinder from the reservoir and also relieves excess pressure when the brakes are released.

pregunta: Which valve in a hydraulic system normally has the highest pressure setting?
explicacion: The thermal relief valve has the highest pressure setting because it protects trapped hydraulic fluid from pressure increases caused by thermal expansion.

pregunta: A multi-disk brake system operates sluggishly and jerks during braking. What corrective action should be taken?
explicacion: Dirty or sticking automatic adjusters commonly cause sluggish and jerky operation in multi-disk brake systems.

pregunta: Proximity sensors are inductive coils that operate in conjunction with:
explicacion: Inductive proximity sensors detect the presence of ferrous metal, typically steel target plates.

pregunta: What component reduces oxygen pressure from the storage bottle before it reaches the pilot?
explicacion: A pressure reducing valve lowers the high bottle pressure to a safe pressure for the aircraft oxygen system.

pregunta: What is dihedral?
opciones: The angle between the wing and the aircraft lateral (pitch) axis
The angle between the wing chord and the relative wind
The angle between the wing and the longitudinal axis
The angle between the vertical stabilizer and the fuselage
explicacion: Dihedral is the upward angle of the wings relative to the aircraft lateral (horizontal) axis, providing lateral stability.

pregunta: An aircraft with good directional stability has less tendency to:
explicacion: Directional stability refers to stability about the vertical axis and resistance to yawing.

pregunta: Where does a stall normally occur first on a wing?
explicacion: Most aircraft are designed so the wing root stalls first, preserving aileron effectiveness near the wing tips.

pregunta: What absorbs the first impact of landing on an aircraft oleo strut?
opciones: Oil flowing through the metering pin
explicacion: The initial landing impact is absorbed by hydraulic oil passing through the metering pin, while the compressed air cushions subsequent movement.

pregunta: What is lateral stability?
explicacion: Lateral stability is stability about the aircraft's longitudinal axis and resists rolling motions.

pregunta: What is the function of the flapper valve in an aircraft fuel tank?
opciones: To act as a check valve and prevent fuel from flowing away from the boost pump during maneuvers
To remove contaminants from the fuel
explicacion: A flapper valve acts as a check valve to keep fuel near the boost pumps during turns, climbs, and other maneuvers.

pregunta: Which valve normally has the highest pressure setting in an aircraft hydraulic system?
explicacion: The thermal relief valve is normally set higher than the system relief valve to protect trapped hydraulic fluid from thermal expansion.

pregunta: Why are tubes in deicer boots inflated alternately?
opciones: To keep airflow disturbance to a minimum
To prevent the boots from freezing
explicacion: Alternating inflation minimizes airflow disturbance while effectively breaking accumulated ice.

pregunta: An airplane with good longitudinal stability has a minimum tendency to:
explicacion: Longitudinal stability is stability about the lateral axis and minimizes pitching tendencies.

pregunta: An airplane is controlled directionally about its vertical axis by the:
explicacion: The rudder controls yaw about the aircraft's vertical axis.

pregunta: What is the formula for wing loading?
explicacion: Wing loading is calculated by dividing the aircraft weight by the wing area.

pregunta: What device reduces crossflow (spanwise flow) over a wing?
explicacion: A wing fence reduces spanwise airflow, improving airflow control and delaying tip stall.

pregunta: What are the two common types of AC motors used in aircraft?
explicacion: The two common AC motor types used in aircraft are induction motors and synchronous motors.

pregunta: Which cabin pressurization mode maintains a constant cabin altitude regardless of changes in aircraft altitude?
explicacion: In the isobaric mode, the cabin pressure altitude remains constant while the aircraft changes altitude, provided the system can maintain that cabin altitude.

pregunta: What are the three basic cabin pressurization modes of operation?
explicacion: The three basic pressurization modes are Differential, Unpressurized, and Isobaric.

pregunta: The pressure required to service aircraft wheels and tires is specified in the:
explicacion: The Aircraft Maintenance Manual (AMM) specifies the correct servicing pressure for aircraft tires.

pregunta: What should be used to clean tire bead seats before installing a tire?
explicacion: Isopropyl alcohol is recommended for cleaning tire bead seats before installation.

pregunta: If phosphate ester-based hydraulic fluid is spilled on an aircraft tire, what should be used to clean it?
explicacion: Phosphate ester fluids such as Skydrol should be washed off tires with soap and water.

pregunta: What is the best safeguard against heat buildup in aircraft tires?
opciones: Short ground rolls, slow taxi speeds, minimum braking, and proper tire inflation
explicacion: Proper operating techniques and correct tire inflation minimize heat buildup.

pregunta: What is Station 0 on an aircraft?
explicacion: Station 0 is the aircraft datum from which all longitudinal measurements are referenced.

pregunta: Long-range communication between an aircraft and a ground station controller is commonly accomplished using:
explicacion: HF communication is commonly used for long-range aircraft-to-ground communication.

pregunta: What unit converts AC generator output into DC current to charge the aircraft battery?
explicacion: Rectifiers convert AC power to DC power for charging the aircraft battery.

pregunta: What two types of electrical loads are normally considered during aircraft electrical load analysis?
explicacion: Electrical load analysis classifies equipment as continuous or intermittent to determine total system demand.

pregunta: During inspection of terminal strips in an aircraft electrical system, it should be verified that:
opciones: Terminal studs are free to rotate
Terminal studs are anchored against rotation
explicacion: Terminal studs must be securely anchored to prevent rotation and damage to the wiring.

pregunta: Air is generally removed from an aircraft hydraulic system by:
opciones: Operating the hydraulic components through several cycles
explicacion: Cycling the hydraulic components allows trapped air to return to the reservoir.

pregunta: Two hydraulic actuating cylinders of different diameters but equal stroke are connected to the same pressure source. They will have:
opciones: Different pressure and the same force
The same pressure but different force
The same force and the same pressure
explicacion: Hydraulic pressure is equal throughout the system, but force depends on piston area (Force = Pressure × Area).

pregunta: In an anti-skid brake system, what happens when one wheel locks during landing?
opciones: Hydraulic pressure to that brake is released
explicacion: The anti-skid system reduces or releases brake pressure to the locked wheel until it resumes rotating.

pregunta: Which instruments are connected to the aircraft pitot-static system?
opciones: Altimeter, Airspeed Indicator, and Vertical Speed Indicator
Attitude Indicator, Heading Indicator, and VSI
Turn Coordinator, Airspeed Indicator, and Altimeter
explicacion: The pitot-static system supplies pressure information to the altimeter, airspeed indicator, and vertical speed indicator.

pregunta: What is the maximum allowable altitude loss during an aircraft static system leakage test?
explicacion: The commonly accepted maximum leakage rate during a static system test is 100 feet per minute.

pregunta: Which component allows the pilot to manually maneuver the aircraft while the autopilot remains engaged?
explicacion: The flight controller allows manual maneuvering while the autopilot remains engaged.

pregunta: Which tab moves in the same direction as the flight control surface?
explicacion: A servo tab moves in the same direction as the control surface to assist pilot input.

pregunta: A conical elastomeric bearing is designed to carry which type of loads?
explicacion: Conical elastomeric bearings are designed to support both high radial and axial loads while providing vibration damping.

pregunta: If the freewheeling unit fails during autorotation, what will occur?
opciones: The engine will continue to drive the main rotor normally
The main rotor will drive the engine
The tail rotor will stop immediately
The rotor brake will automatically engage
explicacion: If the freewheeling unit seizes or fails to disengage during autorotation, the main rotor will drive the engine instead of rotating freely.

pregunta: A helicopter vibration that remains constant throughout the entire RPM range is usually caused by:
explicacion: Chordwise imbalance typically produces a vibration whose amplitude remains nearly constant throughout the operating RPM range.

pregunta: At approximately what airspeed does effective translational lift occur?
explicacion: Effective translational lift is normally experienced between 15 and 20 mph.

pregunta: Most European helicopters require which pedal input when increasing power?
explicacion: Most European helicopters rotate in the opposite direction to American helicopters, requiring right pedal when power is increased.

pregunta: What type of pump is commonly used in high-pressure aircraft hydraulic systems?
explicacion: Piston pumps are used in high-pressure hydraulic systems because they can efficiently produce very high operating pressures.

pregunta: A dual tachometer in a single-engine helicopter indicates:
opciones: Engine RPM and tail rotor RPM
Main rotor RPM and tail rotor RPM
Engine RPM and main rotor RPM
explicacion: A dual tachometer displays both engine RPM and main rotor RPM.

pregunta: During autorotation, airflow through the main rotor is:
explicacion: During autorotation, air flows upward through the rotor system, keeping the rotor turning.

pregunta: Why is a semirigid rotor head underslung?
explicacion: An underslung rotor head reduces stresses caused by blade flapping and improves rotor stability.

pregunta: An overstable helicopter with delayed control response is commonly caused by:
explicacion: A soft stabilizer bar can produce an overstable helicopter with sluggish control response.

pregunta: Which rotor head incorporates flapping hinges?
explicacion: A fully articulated rotor head has individual flapping hinges for each rotor blade.

pregunta: Failure of a rotor damper most commonly results in:
explicacion: A failed lead-lag damper commonly produces a noticeable 1:1 beat or shuttle vibration.

pregunta: Which system is the most widely used for dynamic helicopter rotor balancing?
explicacion: Chadwick-Helmuth equipment is the industry standard for helicopter rotor dynamic balancing.

pregunta: In a hydraulically boosted flight control system, artificial feel is provided by:
explicacion: A gradient unit provides artificial control feel in hydraulic boosted flight control systems.

pregunta: The Coriolis effect in a helicopter results from:
explicacion: As blades flap up and down, their distance from the axis of rotation changes, producing the Coriolis effect (lead-lag motion).

pregunta: The airflow at the rear of the rotor disc having a greater downward angle than at the front is known as:
explicacion: The transverse flow effect results from different induced airflow across the front and rear portions of the rotor disc.

pregunta: What is the purpose of a movable horizontal stabilizer on a helicopter?
opciones: Improve longitudinal stability and increase the center of gravity range
explicacion: A movable horizontal stabilizer improves longitudinal stability and expands the allowable center of gravity range.

pregunta: The horizontal stabilizer on many helicopters is shaped as an:
explicacion: Many helicopter horizontal stabilizers use an inverted asymmetrical airfoil to provide stabilizing forces.

pregunta: Which flight control changes the blade pitch angle continuously throughout each revolution of the rotor disc?
Ground resonance is primarily associated with which rotor head type?
explicacion: Ground resonance is a characteristic hazard of fully articulated rotor systems and results from geometric imbalance.

pregunta: Tail rotor dynamic balancing is commonly performed using:
explicacion: Strobex and Phasor (or similar Chadwick-Helmuth equipment) are commonly used for dynamic tail rotor balancing.

pregunta: What is laminar flow?
opciones: Smooth, orderly airflow in the boundary layer
Airflow moving perpendicular to the wing
explicacion: Laminar flow is smooth, orderly airflow in which adjacent layers of air move parallel with very little mixing.

pregunta: What are the pieces of rubberized fabric that protect aircraft tire beads called?
explicacion: Chafers are layers of rubberized fabric that protect the tire bead from wear and damage where it contacts the wheel.

pregunta: After installing an ADF loop antenna, what maintenance procedure should be performed?
explicacion: After installation, the ADF loop antenna should be calibrated to ensure accurate bearing indications.

pregunta: Which condition could prevent an aircraft landing gear from fully retracting?
explicacion: Insufficient hydraulic fluid may prevent the landing gear actuators from completing the retraction cycle.

pregunta: Which hydraulic component directs fluid to the selected actuator?
explicacion: A selector valve directs hydraulic fluid to the desired actuator for extension or retraction.

pregunta: What is the maximum recommended control cable deflection through a fairlead?
explicacion: A fairlead should not deflect a control cable more than 3° to minimize friction and wear.

pregunta: After a flight control cable is manufactured, what test is performed to verify its strength?
explicacion: A proof load test verifies the cable by applying approximately 60% of its minimum breaking strength without causing permanent deformation.

pregunta: Movement of the ailerons rotates the aircraft about which axis?
explicacion: The ailerons control roll about the aircraft's longitudinal axis.

pregunta: What is load factor?
opciones: The ratio of lift supported by the wings to the aircraft weight
The aircraft weight divided by wing area
The ratio of drag to lift
The total weight carried by the landing gear
explicacion: Load factor is the ratio of the aerodynamic load (lift) supported by the wings to the actual weight of the aircraft, commonly expressed in Gs.

pregunta: Where is the main fuel filter normally located in an aircraft fuel system?
opciones: At the lowest point between the fuel tank and the fuel control unit
Immediately after the engine-driven fuel pump
explicacion: The main fuel filter is normally installed at the lowest practical point between the fuel tank and the fuel control unit to remove contaminants before they reach the engine.

pregunta: What is the purpose of drag and anti-drag wires in a wing structure?
opciones: To transmit loads to the spars and prevent structural distortion
explicacion: Drag and anti-drag wires brace the wing structure, transferring loads to the spars and preventing fore-and-aft distortion.

pregunta: When using a wood auger, what should be placed on the opposite side of the wood?
explicacion: A backing piece of wood helps prevent splintering as the auger exits the workpiece.

pregunta: Air trapped in an aircraft brake hydraulic system will usually cause:
explicacion: Air is compressible, causing the brake pedal to feel soft or spongy and reducing braking effectiveness.

pregunta: The red dot marked on an aircraft tire should be aligned with the:
explicacion: When a tube is installed, the tire's red dot is aligned with the tube's yellow stripe as specified by the manufacturer.

pregunta: The majority of aircraft oxygen systems are:
explicacion: Most transport aircraft use either diluter-demand or pressure-demand oxygen systems.

pregunta: What is the angle of incidence?
opciones: The angle between the wing chord and the relative wind
The angle between the wing chord line and the aircraft longitudinal axis
The angle between the wing and the horizontal plane
The angle between the stabilizer and the fuselage
explicacion: The angle of incidence is the fixed angle between the wing chord line and the aircraft longitudinal axis.

pregunta: How long should you wait after flight before checking aircraft tire pressure?
opciones: At least 2 hours (about 3 hours in warm weather)
explicacion: Tire pressure should be checked after the tires have cooled to obtain an accurate pressure reading.

pregunta: Where does laminar airflow begin on an airfoil?
explicacion: The boundary layer begins at the leading edge and is initially laminar before transitioning to turbulent flow.

pregunta: When the elevator is deflected downward, the aircraft nose will:
explicacion: Deflecting the elevator downward increases tail lift, causing the aircraft nose to pitch upward.

pregunta: What is the angle of attack?
opciones: The angle between the wing chord line and the aircraft longitudinal axis
The angle between the wing chord line and the relative wind
The angle between the wing and the horizontal plane
The angle between the stabilizer and the relative wind
explicacion: Angle of attack is the angle between the wing chord line and the relative wind.

pregunta: What is the purpose of vortex generators?
opciones: Energize the boundary layer to delay airflow separation
explicacion: Vortex generators create small vortices that bring high-energy air into the boundary layer, delaying airflow separation and improving control effectiveness at high angles of attack.

pregunta: What is monocoque construction?
opciones: A structure in which the skin carries most of the structural loads
A structure supported entirely by internal trusses
A structure using only bulkheads for strength
A structure reinforced exclusively with stringers
explicacion: In monocoque construction, the skin carries most of the structural loads with minimal internal reinforcement.

pregunta: What does an aircraft autopilot primarily sense?
opciones: Aircraft attitude and deviation from the selected flight path
explicacion: The autopilot receives information from gyros, attitude sensors, heading systems, navigation sources, and air data to detect deviations from the selected flight path.

pregunta: Why are the pole shoes of a DC generator laminated?
explicacion: Laminating the pole shoes reduces eddy current losses, improving generator efficiency.

pregunta: Why are Micarta fairleads commonly used for aircraft control cables?
opciones: They wear instead of the control cable
explicacion: Micarta fairleads are softer than steel control cables, allowing the fairlead to wear instead of damaging the cable.

pregunta: The air used for cabin pressurization in most turbine aircraft is supplied from:
explicacion: Most turbine aircraft use compressed engine bleed air as the source of cabin pressurization.

pregunta: If the cabin rate of climb is excessive, what adjustment should be made?
explicacion: Closing the outflow valve more quickly slows the cabin rate of climb by retaining cabin pressure.

pregunta: In the isobaric mode, what happens if the isobaric metering valve moves off its seat?
explicacion: When the isobaric metering valve moves off its seat, additional reference air is vented, causing the outflow valve to open and cabin pressure to decrease.

pregunta: How is cabin pressure maintained during isobaric operation?
opciones: By continuously increasing bleed air pressure
By modulating the outflow valve in response to reference chamber pressure
By cycling the safety valve open and closed
explicacion: The isobaric control system senses changes in reference chamber pressure and modulates the outflow valve to maintain a constant cabin pressure.

pregunta: Why are wings swept back on high-speed aircraft?
opciones: To delay the onset of compressibility effects at high speeds
explicacion: Swept wings reduce the component of airflow perpendicular to the leading edge, delaying critical Mach effects and improving high-speed performance.

pregunta: What are the three primary flight controls of an aircraft?
explicacion: The three primary flight controls are the ailerons (roll), elevators (pitch), and rudder (yaw).

pregunta: Which aircraft instrument requires both pitot and static pressure to operate?
explicacion: The airspeed indicator compares pitot pressure with static pressure to determine the aircraft's indicated airspeed.

pregunta: As the angle of attack increases, the center of pressure generally moves:
explicacion: For most conventional airfoils operating below the critical angle of attack, the center of pressure moves forward as the angle of attack increases.

pregunta: If an aircraft consistently flies left-wing heavy, which adjustment would help correct the condition?
opciones: Wash in the left wing or wash out the right wing
Wash out the left wing only
explicacion: A wing-heavy condition can often be corrected by adjusting the wing rigging, such as washing in the heavy wing or washing out the opposite wing.

pregunta: What is wing loading?
opciones: Aircraft maximum gross weight divided by the total wing area
Wing span squared divided by wing area
Aircraft weight divided by fuselage length
explicacion: Wing loading is calculated by dividing the aircraft's weight by the total wing area.

pregunta: If flight control cables are rigged too tight, what is the most likely result?
explicacion: Excessively tight control cables increase friction and control forces, resulting in heavy flight controls.

pregunta: A wing that has a greater angle of incidence at the root than at the tip is commonly called:
explicacion: Wash-in means the wing root has a greater angle of incidence than the wing tip.

pregunta: What is the primary purpose of washout on a wing?
opciones: To ensure the wing root stalls before the wing tip
explicacion: Washout reduces the angle of incidence toward the wing tip, helping the wing root stall first and maintaining aileron effectiveness.

pregunta: Why are generator pole shoes laminated?
explicacion: Laminating generator pole shoes minimizes eddy current losses and improves generator efficiency.

pregunta: The primary source of cabin pressurization air in turbine-powered aircraft is:
explicacion: Compressed bleed air extracted from the engine compressor section is the primary source of cabin pressurization in turbine aircraft.

pregunta: In autorotation, the American tail rotor normally has:
explicacion: In autorotation, the tail rotor may operate with negative pitch depending on rotor direction and anti-torque requirements.

pregunta: A conical elastomeric bearing is designed to carry:
explicacion: Conical elastomeric bearings are designed to carry both high radial and axial loads.

pregunta: What type of bearing is commonly used on the end of pitch links?
explicacion: Pitch links commonly use spherical bearings to allow angular movement during blade pitch changes.

pregunta: What forces act on a helicopter mast?
explicacion: A helicopter mast is subjected mainly to tension and torsion loads from rotor lift and torque transmission.

pregunta: A battery that delivers 7.0 amperes for 5 hours has what capacity?
explicacion: Battery capacity = current × time = 7.0 A × 5 h = 35 ampere-hours.

pregunta: What kind of antenna is commonly used for VOR?
explicacion: A VOR receiver commonly uses a special form of dipole antenna, often called a V-type or cat-whisker antenna.

pregunta: For vertical flight at a constant upward rate, the relationship of forces is:
opciones: Thrust plus lift is greater than weight plus drag
Weight plus drag is greater than thrust plus lift
explicacion: For a vertical ascent, thrust/lift must overcome the opposing forces of weight and drag.

pregunta: The altimeter setting is adjusted using:
explicacion: The altimeter is set using the local barometric pressure setting.

pregunta: A lateral vibration in a helicopter is commonly caused by:
explicacion: Chordwise imbalance commonly produces lateral vibration in a helicopter.

pregunta: In a semirigid rotor, the effects of advancing and retreating blades are compensated by:
explicacion: A semirigid rotor uses a seesaw teetering action to compensate for dissymmetry of lift between advancing and retreating blades.

pregunta: On a free-turbine helicopter engine, what does the twist grip control?
explicacion: On most free-turbine helicopter engines, the twist grip controls engine (gas generator/compressor) RPM.

pregunta: If the freewheeling unit fails to disengage during autorotation, what will occur?
opciones: The engine will drive the rotor
The main rotor will drive the engine
explicacion: If the freewheeling unit fails, the main rotor remains mechanically connected and drives the engine during autorotation.

pregunta: What is the angle of attack?
opciones: The angle between the wing chord line and the relative wind
The angle between the wing chord line and the aircraft longitudinal axis
The angle between the wing and the horizontal plane
The angle between the stabilizer and the fuselage
explicacion: Angle of attack is the acute angle between the chord line of an airfoil and the relative wind.

pregunta: Compared with the retreating blade, the advancing rotor blade normally has:
explicacion: The advancing blade experiences higher airspeed, so it operates at a lower angle of attack than the retreating blade.

pregunta: Lead-lag hinges are characteristic of which rotor head type?
explicacion: Fully articulated rotor heads incorporate lead-lag and flapping hinges for each blade.

pregunta: The amount of rotor blade coning is primarily determined by the relationship between:
opciones: Rotor RPM and tail rotor thrust
explicacion: Coning angle is determined by the balance between upward aerodynamic lift and outward centrifugal force.

pregunta: A helicopter vibration that remains nearly constant throughout the RPM range usually indicates:
explicacion: Chordwise imbalance typically produces a vibration whose amplitude changes very little with rotor RPM.

pregunta: What is the function of an inverter in an aircraft electrical system?
explicacion: An inverter converts direct current (DC) into alternating current (AC).

pregunta: During an ILS approach, which component provides lateral guidance to align the aircraft with the runway?
explicacion: The localizer provides left-right (lateral) guidance to align the aircraft with the runway centerline.

pregunta: The Vertical Speed Indicator (VSI) operates using:
explicacion: The VSI measures the rate of change of static pressure to indicate the aircraft's rate of climb or descent.

pregunta: At approximately what airspeed does effective translational lift become noticeable?
explicacion: Effective translational lift (ETL) is normally experienced between 15 and 20 mph.

pregunta: A vertical vibration in a helicopter is most commonly caused by:
explicacion: Rotor blades that are out of track produce a characteristic vertical vibration.

pregunta: Which components make up a conventional Instrument Landing System (ILS)?
opciones: Localizer, Glide Slope and Marker Beacons
explicacion: A conventional ILS consists of a localizer, glide slope, and marker beacons.

pregunta: A hydraulic priority valve is normally operated by:
explicacion: Priority valves are hydraulically actuated to ensure essential systems receive pressure first.

pregunta: Most European helicopters require which pedal input when power is increased?
explicacion: Most European helicopters rotate opposite to most U.S.-built helicopters, requiring right pedal when power is increased.

pregunta: What type of pump is commonly used in high-pressure aircraft hydraulic systems?
explicacion: Piston pumps are capable of producing the high pressures required in aircraft hydraulic systems.

pregunta: What is the primary function of a hydraulic pump?
explicacion: A hydraulic pump creates flow. Pressure is produced only when the flow encounters resistance.

pregunta: In forward flight, a helicopter turns primarily by:
opciones: Tilting the rotor disc with cyclic control
explicacion: The cyclic control tilts the rotor disc, changing the direction of the lift vector and turning the helicopter.

pregunta: A dual tachometer in a single-engine helicopter normally indicates:
opciones: Main rotor RPM and tail rotor RPM
Engine RPM and main rotor RPM
Main rotor RPM and oil pressure
explicacion: The dual tachometer displays both engine RPM and main rotor RPM.

pregunta: Why are composite helicopter rotor blades often preferred over metal blades?
opciones: They have a longer service life
explicacion: Composite rotor blades generally provide a longer service life due to improved fatigue and corrosion resistance.

pregunta: What is the torque effect in a helicopter?
opciones: The fuselage turns in the same direction as the main rotor
Force and reaction cause the fuselage to rotate opposite the main rotor
explicacion: According to Newton's Third Law, the fuselage tends to rotate in the opposite direction of the main rotor.

pregunta: During autorotation, airflow through the main rotor system is:
explicacion: During autorotation, air flows upward through the rotor system, keeping the rotor turning.

pregunta: Why is a semirigid rotor head underslung?
explicacion: The underslung design reduces stresses caused by blade flapping and improves rotor stability.

pregunta: The collective control changes the pitch of the rotor blades:
explicacion: The collective changes the pitch angle equally on all rotor blades, increasing or decreasing total rotor thrust.

pregunta: A hard rotor damper generally results in:
opciones: A fast response but less stable helicopter
explicacion: A hard damper increases control response but can make the helicopter less stable.

pregunta: The drive links connect the:
opciones: Tail rotor gearbox to the driveshaft
Rotating swashplate to the rotor mast
explicacion: Drive links transmit cyclic and collective inputs from the rotating swashplate to the rotor hub.

pregunta: An isolation valve in a hydraulic system is commonly operated:
explicacion: Many isolation valves are mechanically actuated to isolate sections of the hydraulic system.

pregunta: The Vertical Speed Indicator (VSI) measures the rate of climb or descent using:
explicacion: The VSI senses the rate of change of static pressure to indicate vertical speed.

pregunta: What is the purpose of a freewheeling unit in a helicopter?
opciones: Disconnect the engine from the main rotor during autorotation
explicacion: The freewheeling unit allows the rotor system to continue turning independently of the engine during autorotation.

pregunta: Which rotor head incorporates flapping hinges?
explicacion: A fully articulated rotor head uses individual flapping hinges for each rotor blade.

pregunta: A fully articulated rotor head incorporates which type of hinge?
explicacion: Each blade of a fully articulated rotor head has a flapping hinge that allows independent vertical movement.

pregunta: Failure of a rotor damper most commonly produces:
explicacion: A failed lead-lag damper commonly produces a noticeable 1-per-revolution (1:1) beat or shuffle.

pregunta: Most turbine helicopter fuel systems are:
explicacion: Most turbine helicopters use forced-feed fuel systems to ensure a continuous supply of fuel under all flight conditions.

pregunta: What is the most widely used helicopter dynamic balancing system?
explicacion: The Chadwick-Helmuth system is one of the most widely used helicopter rotor dynamic balancing systems.

pregunta: In a closed hydraulic system, hydraulic pressure is:
opciones: Constant while force varies with piston area
explicacion: Hydraulic pressure remains essentially constant throughout the system, while output force depends on piston area.

pregunta: On many helicopters, Station X commonly refers to the:
explicacion: Station X is commonly used to identify the front jacking point on many helicopter maintenance drawings.

pregunta: In a hydraulically boosted flight control system, artificial feel is normally provided by:
explicacion: A gradient unit provides artificial control feel so the pilot can sense control forces in a hydraulically boosted system.

pregunta: The magnitude of the rotor blade coning angle depends primarily on the relationship between:
explicacion: Coning angle increases as aerodynamic lift increases relative to centrifugal force.

pregunta: Why are semirigid rotor systems designed with an underslung rotor head?
opciones: To reduce the effects of Coriolis forces during blade flapping
explicacion: The underslung design minimizes changes in the center of mass during blade flapping, reducing Coriolis-induced stresses.

pregunta: Where is the helicopter rotor brake normally installed?
opciones: Between the engine and the main transmission input
explicacion: The rotor brake is typically mounted between the engine output and the main transmission input shaft.

pregunta: What is the primary purpose of blade twist on a helicopter rotor blade?
opciones: To improve lift distribution along the blade
explicacion: Blade twist provides a more uniform angle of attack from root to tip, improving lift distribution and rotor efficiency.

pregunta: The Coriolis effect causes the blade center of mass to:
opciones: Move farther away from the axis of rotation
Move closer to the axis of rotation during flapping
Remain fixed relative to the hub
explicacion: During blade flapping, the blade's center of mass moves closer to or farther from the axis of rotation, producing the Coriolis (lead-lag) effect.

pregunta: The Coriolis effect in a helicopter rotor system is primarily caused by:
explicacion: Blade flapping changes the blade's distance from the axis of rotation, producing lead-lag motion known as the Coriolis effect.

pregunta: The greater downward airflow at the rear of the rotor disc compared with the front is known as:
explicacion: The transverse flow effect results from unequal induced airflow across the rotor disc during forward flight.

pregunta: What is the primary purpose of a movable horizontal stabilizer on a helicopter?
opciones: Improve longitudinal stability and extend the center of gravity range
explicacion: A movable horizontal stabilizer improves longitudinal stability and increases the allowable center of gravity range.

pregunta: The horizontal stabilizer on many helicopters is shaped as an:
explicacion: Many helicopters use an inverted asymmetrical airfoil to provide stabilizing downforce during forward flight.

pregunta: Which flight control continuously changes rotor blade pitch during each revolution of the rotor disc?
explicacion: The cyclic control changes blade pitch at specific points during each revolution, tilting the rotor disc.

pregunta: Ground resonance is primarily associated with which rotor system?
explicacion: Ground resonance is a phenomenon associated primarily with fully articulated rotor systems and results from geometric imbalance.

pregunta: Which equipment is commonly used to dynamically balance helicopter tail rotor blades?
explicacion: Strobex is commonly used for dynamic balancing and tracking of helicopter rotor systems.

pregunta: Blade alignment on a semirigid rotor system is commonly referred to as:
explicacion: Blade alignment on semirigid rotor systems is commonly referred to as chordwise balance.

pregunta: Vertical flight of a helicopter is primarily controlled by:
explicacion: The collective changes the pitch of all main rotor blades equally, increasing or decreasing total lift.

pregunta: The tendency of anti-torque pedals to slowly move away from the neutral position is called:
explicacion: Pedal creep is the gradual movement of the anti-torque pedals away from neutral due to hydraulic servo characteristics.

pregunta: The torque effect causes the helicopter fuselage to:
opciones: Rotate in the same direction as the main rotor
Rotate opposite to the direction of the main rotor
explicacion: According to Newton's Third Law, the fuselage tends to rotate opposite the direction of the main rotor.

pregunta: On most U.S.-built helicopters, increasing left anti-torque pedal will:
explicacion: On most U.S.-built helicopters, pressing the left pedal increases tail rotor blade pitch, increasing anti-torque thrust.

pregunta: During manufacture, each wooden rotor blade is matched to:
opciones: A master blade and its matching blade
explicacion: Wooden rotor blades are carefully matched to a master blade and to their companion blade to ensure balance.

pregunta: Regardless of the balancing method used, helicopter rotor balancing always includes:
explicacion: Rotor balancing always includes spanwise and chordwise balance before dynamic balancing is performed.

pregunta: On a metal helicopter rotor blade, the skin primarily:
opciones: Provides the aerodynamic shape by wrapping around the internal structure
explicacion: The metal skin wraps around the blade structure to form its aerodynamic profile.

pregunta: Which helicopter rotor system is susceptible to mast bumping?
explicacion: Mast bumping is primarily associated with semirigid (teetering) rotor systems.

pregunta: The rotor brake on many helicopters is supplied by:
explicacion: Many helicopter rotor brakes are powered by an independent hydraulic system.

pregunta: A helicopter that is very stable but slow to respond to control inputs most likely has:
opciones: A soft stabilizer bar or soft damper
explicacion: A soft stabilizer bar or soft damper results in an overstable helicopter with delayed control response.

pregunta: For a helicopter climbing vertically at a constant rate, the combined thrust and lift must be:
opciones: Equal to the combined weight and drag
Greater than the combined weight and drag
Less than the combined weight and drag
explicacion: To maintain a positive rate of climb, the upward forces (thrust/lift) must exceed the opposing forces (weight and drag).

pregunta: The magnitude of the rotor blade coning angle is primarily determined by the relationship between:
opciones: Lift produced by the blade and centrifugal force
Main rotor and tail rotor thrust
explicacion: Coning angle is established by the balance between centrifugal force pulling the blade outward and aerodynamic lift pulling it upward.

pregunta: Most turbine-powered helicopters use what type of fuel system?
explicacion: Most turbine helicopters use a forced-feed fuel system to provide a continuous fuel supply under all operating conditions.

pregunta: What is the primary purpose of a horizontal stabilizer on a helicopter?
opciones: Improve longitudinal stability and increase the allowable center of gravity range
explicacion: The horizontal stabilizer improves pitch stability and extends the allowable CG range during forward flight.

pregunta: A helicopter rotor system that is out of track will most commonly produce:
explicacion: Out-of-track rotor blades produce a vertical vibration because the blades are not rotating in the same plane.

pregunta: A helicopter rotor with chordwise imbalance typically produces:
explicacion: Chordwise imbalance typically results in a lateral vibration that remains relatively constant throughout the RPM range.

pregunta: What is the Coriolis effect in a helicopter rotor system?
opciones: Blade lead and lag caused by changes in the blade's distance from the axis of rotation during flapping
Unequal lift between the advancing and retreating blades
Vertical vibration caused by blade tracking errors
The tendency of the fuselage to rotate opposite the main rotor
explicacion: As a rotor blade flaps, its center of mass moves closer to or farther from the mast. To conserve angular momentum, the blade leads or lags. This is known as the Coriolis effect.

pregunta: What motion is commonly associated with the Coriolis effect?
explicacion: The Coriolis effect causes the blades to lead and lag (hunt fore and aft) as they flap.

pregunta: What causes a helicopter fuselage to rotate opposite the direction of the main rotor?
explicacion: Torque reaction is the equal and opposite reaction produced by the rotating main rotor, as described by Newton's Third Law.

pregunta: What type of vibration is most commonly associated with rotor blades that are out of track?
explicacion: Rotor blades that do not rotate in the same plane produce a characteristic vertical vibration.

pregunta: A helicopter rotor with chordwise imbalance will normally produce:
explicacion: Chordwise imbalance typically produces a lateral vibration whose amplitude changes very little with rotor RPM.

pregunta: What is the primary purpose of blade twist in a helicopter rotor blade?
opciones: Improve lift distribution from root to tip
explicacion: Blade twist compensates for the increase in rotational speed toward the blade tip, resulting in a more uniform angle of attack and lift distribution.

pregunta: The artificial feel system in a hydraulically boosted helicopter flight control system is usually provided by a:
explicacion: A gradient unit provides artificial control forces so the pilot can feel control inputs despite hydraulic boosting.

pregunta: Which helicopter rotor system is most susceptible to mast bumping?
explicacion: Mast bumping is a hazard associated primarily with semirigid (teetering) rotor systems.

pregunta: The transverse flow effect is caused by:
opciones: Unequal induced airflow across the rotor disc during forward flight
explicacion: In forward flight, the rear portion of the rotor disc experiences greater induced flow than the front, producing the transverse flow effect.

pregunta: The horizontal stabilizer on a helicopter primarily improves:
explicacion: The horizontal stabilizer improves longitudinal stability and helps maintain proper pitch attitude during forward flight.

pregunta: The Coriolis effect causes rotor blades to:
opciones: Lead and lag as they flap
explicacion: As a blade flaps, its distance from the axis of rotation changes. To conserve angular momentum, the blade leads or lags. This is known as the Coriolis effect.

pregunta: Angle of attack is defined as the angle between the:
opciones: Wing chord line and the relative wind
Wing chord line and the aircraft longitudinal axis
Relative wind and the aircraft longitudinal axis
explicacion: Angle of attack is the acute angle formed between the chord line of an airfoil and the relative wind.

pregunta: Rotor blade coning occurs because of the balance between:
explicacion: The coning angle is established by the balance between aerodynamic lift acting upward and centrifugal force acting outward.

pregunta: What could cause a chordwise imbalance in a semirigid rotor head?
explicacion: On many semirigid rotor systems, an incorrectly adjusted drag brace link can produce a chordwise imbalance and lateral vibration.

pregunta: What is the primary function of a fixed horizontal stabilizer on a helicopter?
opciones: Pull the tail downward to improve longitudinal stability
explicacion: A fixed horizontal stabilizer produces a downward aerodynamic force on the tail, improving longitudinal stability during forward flight.

pregunta: What type of airfoil is commonly used for a helicopter horizontal stabilizer?
explicacion: Many helicopter horizontal stabilizers use an inverted airfoil to generate a stabilizing downward force in forward flight.

pregunta: Why are helicopter rotor blades permitted to flap?
opciones: To compensate for dissymmetry of lift
explicacion: Blade flapping compensates for dissymmetry of lift between the advancing and retreating blades.

pregunta: What is dissymmetry of lift?
opciones: The unequal lift produced by the advancing and retreating rotor blades
The unequal weight of rotor blades
The difference in tail rotor thrust
The unequal pitch of the main rotor blades
explicacion: In forward flight, the advancing blade experiences higher relative airspeed than the retreating blade, producing unequal lift.

pregunta: On many metal helicopter rotor blades, the skin is attached to the spar by:
explicacion: Many metal rotor blades use bonded construction, where the skin is bonded directly to the spar.

pregunta: What is translational lift?
opciones: Additional lift obtained as the helicopter moves into undisturbed air
Lift produced by the tail rotor
explicacion: Effective translational lift occurs when the rotor system moves into clean, undisturbed air, increasing rotor efficiency.

pregunta: What is the primary purpose of tracking helicopter rotor blades?
opciones: Ensure all blades rotate in the same tip-path plane
explicacion: Blade tracking ensures all rotor blades follow the same tip-path plane, reducing vibration.

pregunta: When the collective pitch lever is raised, what must also increase to maintain rotor RPM?
explicacion: Increasing collective pitch increases blade drag, requiring additional engine power to maintain rotor RPM.

pregunta: When the collective pitch lever is lowered, the rotor blades will:
opciones: Move to a lower pitch angle
explicacion: Lowering the collective decreases the pitch angle of all main rotor blades, reducing lift.

pregunta: What is hunting in a helicopter rotor system?
opciones: Vertical movement of the rotor blade
Horizontal lead-lag movement of the rotor blade about its vertical hinge
explicacion: Hunting is the lead-lag (fore-and-aft) movement of the rotor blade about its drag hinge, primarily caused by the Coriolis effect.

pregunta: What could cause a centrifugal clutch to engage at too low an engine speed?
explicacion: Weak or broken clutch springs allow the centrifugal clutch to engage before the proper engine RPM is reached.

pregunta: In a fully articulated rotor system, blade flapping is primarily a result of:
opciones: Aerodynamic forces acting on the blades
explicacion: Aerodynamic forces acting on the rotor blades cause them to flap up and down about their flapping hinges.

pregunta: When cyclic control is applied to a rotating rotor system, the maximum rotor disc displacement occurs:
opciones: At the point of control input
explicacion: Due to gyroscopic precession, the maximum rotor response occurs approximately 90° later in the direction of rotation.

pregunta: What is the most likely cause of anti-torque pedal creep during a ground run?
explicacion: Excessively heavy counterweights in the control system can contribute to pedal creep during ground operation.

pregunta: What is usually the first indication of a misaligned tail rotor shaft bearing?
explicacion: Bearing overheating is often the earliest indication of shaft misalignment.

pregunta: An improperly balanced tail rotor will most likely produce:
explicacion: Tail rotors operate at high RPM, so imbalance is normally felt as a high-frequency vibration.

pregunta: What action is required if a metal helicopter rotor blade has a 1/8-inch crack?
explicacion: A crack of this magnitude in a metal rotor blade requires blade replacement in accordance with manufacturer limits.

pregunta: What is the primary purpose of a stabilizer bar on a semirigid rotor system?
opciones: Provide a reference for controlling blade angle of attack
explicacion: The stabilizer bar acts as an aerodynamic reference, helping stabilize the rotor disc and improve control response.

pregunta: The freewheeling unit in a helicopter allows:
opciones: The engine to drive the rotor during autorotation
The rotor to rotate independently of the engine
The rotor brake to engage automatically
The tail rotor to overspeed safely
explicacion: The freewheeling unit disconnects the engine from the rotor system during autorotation, allowing the rotor to continue turning freely.

pregunta: If the center of gravity is outside the approved limits, the aircraft may:
explicacion: An aircraft with its center of gravity outside the approved limits may have unsafe handling characteristics and become uncontrollable.

pregunta: What is the reference point for all aircraft weight and balance measurements?
explicacion: The datum is the established reference point from which all arms and moments are measured.

pregunta: How is moment determined in aircraft weight and balance calculations?
explicacion: Moment is calculated by multiplying the item's weight by its arm (Moment = Weight × Arm).

pregunta: How is the aircraft center of gravity calculated?
explicacion: The center of gravity is found by dividing the total moment by the total weight.

pregunta: When using a straight hand reamer, what is the recommended procedure?
opciones: Back the reamer out periodically to clear the flutes
explicacion: A hand reamer should be backed out periodically to remove chips from the flutes and prevent damage to the hole.

pregunta: Aircraft control travel is normally limited by:
explicacion: Control travel is limited by mechanical stops that may be fixed or adjustable depending on the aircraft design.

pregunta: Why is a small inspection hole drilled in an adjustable control rod end?
opciones: To verify proper thread engagement for safety
explicacion: The inspection hole allows maintenance personnel to verify that the threaded rod has been screwed in far enough to ensure safe engagement.

pregunta: When installing a fiber-lock nut on a bolt or stud, how many threads should extend beyond the nut?
explicacion: At least three threads should extend beyond a fiber-lock nut to ensure the locking element is fully engaged.

pregunta: Why should vapor degreasing, steam cleaning, MEK, or similar solvents not be used on flight control cables?
opciones: They remove the cable's internal lubricant
explicacion: Strong solvents remove the internal lubricant from control cables, greatly reducing their service life.

pregunta: When must an aircraft flight control cable be replaced?
opciones: Only when one or more strands are broken
Whenever it is distorted, worn, or corroded, even if no strands are broken
Only after reaching the manufacturer's recommended service life
explicacion: A control cable must be replaced if it is distorted, worn, or corroded, even when no broken strands are present.

pregunta: If the battery relay chatters when the battery master switch is turned ON, the most likely cause is:
explicacion: A weak battery may not provide enough voltage to hold the relay closed, causing it to chatter.

pregunta: When two or more AC generators operate on the same aircraft bus, they must have the same:
explicacion: AC generators operating in parallel must be synchronized in voltage, frequency, and phase sequence.

pregunta: Aircraft electrical wire size is selected primarily based on:
opciones: Current-carrying capacity and allowable voltage drop
explicacion: Wire size is selected to safely carry the required current while keeping voltage drop within acceptable limits.

pregunta: How is an overcharging aircraft battery normally corrected?
explicacion: Battery overcharging is normally corrected by adjusting or replacing the voltage regulator.

pregunta: The master switch in a DC electrical system normally connects the:
opciones: External power unit to the battery
explicacion: The battery master switch connects the aircraft battery to the main DC bus.

pregunta: A split-bus relay may be energized by:
opciones: The battery or an external power source
explicacion: Split-bus relays are commonly energized by either the aircraft battery or an external power source.

pregunta: Why must the output of two DC generators operating in parallel be properly balanced?
opciones: To prevent reverse current flow between generators
explicacion: Balanced generator output prevents one generator from motoring or receiving reverse current from the other.

pregunta: How is the fire warning indicator light normally tested?
opciones: Pressing the outer ring of the warning light
explicacion: Most aircraft fire warning lights incorporate a push-to-test feature by pressing the outer ring.

pregunta: Which flight instrument does NOT require static pressure for operation?
explicacion: The Turn-and-Bank Indicator is gyro driven and does not use the pitot-static system.

pregunta: During a compass swing, if the aircraft is heading north and the sighting compass reads 354° while the aircraft compass reads 356°, the aircraft compass should be adjusted to indicate:
explicacion: During compass compensation, north and east headings are adjusted to remove all error, so the aircraft compass should match the reference compass.

pregunta: As laminar airflow moves toward the trailing edge of an airfoil, what happens to the boundary layer?
opciones: It becomes thicker and gradually transitions to turbulent flow
